name: Frozen Greek-Yogurt Protein Bars
description: Frozen yogurt mold bars: nonfat Greek yogurt whipped with chocolate protein and butterscotch SF pudding mix, folded with crushed Ghost cinnamon cereal, set in candy-bar molds, topped with more cereal and a drizzle of zero-sugar chocolate syrup. Freezer-set, crunchy, very lean. Distinct from the cookie-dough/cheesecake bars — a yogurt-base format closer to the Protein Butterscotch Yogurt Pockets.

steps.instructionText: Whisk the Greek yogurt, milk, butterscotch pudding mix, and chocolate protein until smooth. Crush about half the cereal and fold it in.
Pour into candy-bar molds. Sprinkle the remaining cereal on top and drizzle with the zero-sugar chocolate syrup.
Freeze 6-12 hr until firm, then unmold. Makes 6 bars.

notes.text: The butterscotch SF pudding mix and 2 scoops of chocolate protein thicken fast once whisked — the 15 g of skim milk is the working liquid; don't over-add or the bars won't set firm enough to unmold cleanly.
Crush only about half the Ghost cereal into the batter; leave the top-layer cereal as full pieces so it stays crunchy even after freezing — fully crushed cereal just absorbs moisture and disappears.
Freeze the full 12 hr the first time to ensure a clean unmold — at 6 hr the yogurt center may still be soft and bars will tear coming out of the mold. Once unmolded, they can be stored in a bag.
Pull bars from the freezer 3-4 min before eating; rock-frozen Greek-yogurt bars are dense and hard enough to hurt a tooth — a short temper makes the center creamy without losing the shape.
